# Support matrix

This file is generated from `hca.support`, which is exercised by release tests.
It distinguishes portable core support, optimized telemetry, and unsupported scope.

**Verified stable Hermes versions:** `0.18.2`, `2026.7.7.2`

## Platforms

| Platform | Status | Evidence boundary |
|---|---|---|
| Linux / Python 3.11–3.12 | Supported | Required unit, contract, integration, and clean-wheel CI lanes. |
| Single DGX Spark / GB10 | Optimized | Core lifecycle and conservative telemetry acceptance run on one GB10. |
| macOS | Portable CI | Portable control-plane smoke only; no GB10/device acceptance claim. |

## Capabilities

| Capability | Status | Boundary |
|---|---|---|
| Single-host Hermes team orchestration | Supported | Workers, Kanban task truth, and HCA state live on one host. |
| Remote model endpoint | Supported | Configured by the selected Hermes profile; HCA does not own credentials or fallback. |
| vLLM/SGLang telemetry adapters | Optimized optional | Admission signals only; HCA does not provision or serve models. |
| Unknown or unavailable telemetry | Supported conservatively | Never interpreted as unlimited capacity. |
| Remote agent placement | Unsupported | No supported remote Kanban claim/heartbeat transport; mutation fails before SSH. |

## Release evidence

| Lane | Gate | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| Deterministic goal-to-result lifecycle | Required | Gated in ordinary CI. |
| Pinned stable Hermes private-API contract | Required | Hermes 0.18.2 / 2026.7.7.2, immutable upstream commit verified in CI. |
| Latest Hermes main | Advisory | Visible drift signal; not a masked stable-release gate. |
| Performance | Workload-bound | No universal worker count or speed claim; measure each model/runtime/profile. |

Remote inference is not remote agent placement. A model endpoint may be on another
machine through ordinary Hermes profile configuration, while HCA workers and the
authoritative Kanban board remain colocated.
